person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Edmund Butler, Earl of Carrick and 6th Chief Butler of Ireland (1268-September 13 1321) was a noble in the Peerage of Ireland. He was the second son of Theobald Butler, 4th Chief Butler of Ireland. Edmund went on pilgrimage to Santiago de Compostela in 1321 but died in London on September 13, 1321. He was buried in St. Mary's Collegiate Church Gowran, County Kilkenny on the 10th of November 1321.

«b»Career«/b»
Edmund succeeded to his father's lands upon the death of his elder brother Theobald, the 5th Chief Butler of Ireland, in 1299. He was created Justiciar of Ireland in 1303 with a fee of £500 per annum. In 1309 was knighted by Edward II in London. Three years later he defeated the O'Byrne and O'Toole clans in Glenmalure.

At a great feast in Dublin on Sunday 29 of September 1313, he created 30 Knights, by patent, dated at Langley 4 January 1314.

Having distinguished himself during the Bruce campaign in Ireland alongside John de Bermingham, 1st Earl of Louth and Roger Mortimer, Edmund was granted a charter of the castle and manor of Karryk Macgryffin and Roscrea to hold to him and his heirs sub nomine et honore comitis de Karryk. The patent was dated at Lincoln 1 September that year, 1315; on that date, he was given the return of all the King's writs in the cantreds of Oreman (sic Ormond), Elyogerth (sic Eliogarty), and Elyocarroll in County Tipperary. To these was added, on 12 November 1320, all the lands of William de Carran in Finagh and Favmolin in County Waterford.

However, the charter, while creating an earldom, failed to make Edmund's heir James Earl of Carrick. James was later created Earl of Ormond (Ireland) in his own right in 1328 alongside Roger Mortimer, who was created Earl of March, and the newly created John of Eltham, Earl of Cornwall), brother of King Edward III.

In 1317, after suffering a military defeat in a rebellion led by Edward Bruce, brother of Robert Bruce, he was replaced as Justiciar by Roger Mortimer, 1st Earl of March.

«b»Marriage and Children«/b»
He married Joan FitzGerald in 1302, daughter of the John FitzGerald, 1st Earl of Kildare and together they had several children, the eldest of whom succeeded him as Chief Butler of Ireland but not as Earl of Carrick.

1.) James Butler, 1st Earl of Ormond (1304-1338)

2.) John Butler of Clonamicklon (or Limallon) (c. 1305-1330) was the ancestor of the later creation of Viscount Ikerrin and Earl of Carrick (Ireland). By his wife Johanna, he had issue, Edmond.

3.) Lawrence Butler (1306-January 6 1338)

4.) Joan Butler (1309- November 3 1405) who was married in 1321 to Roger Mortimer (second son of Roger, brother to Edward, Earl of March.)

5.) Margaret who married Sir Thomas Dillon of Drumrany, ancestor to Viscount Dillon.

6.) Alice (1290-March 15 1356)

7.) William ( September 8 1296-1361)

sourceList.title: Sir Edmund Butler, Justiciar of Ireland (1273-1321), Our Royal, Titled, Noble, and Commoner Ancestors

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Edmund Butler Earl Of Carrick於1271年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 基爾堅尼基爾堅尼時，他的父親Theobald Butler 4th Chief Butler of Ireland29歲，母親Joan FitzJohn-33歲。他於1295年與Joan Fitz Gerald Countess Of Carrick在英國英格蘭素瑞黑結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他於1303年在愛爾蘭被列為justiciar of ireland。他去世於1321年9月13日享年50歲，埋葬在愛爾蘭庫迪 基爾堅尼高萊安。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Edmund Butler Earl Of Carrick于1271年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 基爾堅尼基爾堅尼时，他的父亲Theobald Butler 4th Chief Butler of Ireland29岁，母亲Joan FitzJohn-33岁。他于1295年与Joan Fitz Gerald Countess Of Carrick在英国英格兰素瑞黑结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他于1303年在爱尔兰被列为justiciar of ireland。他去世于1321年9月13日享年50岁，埋葬在爱尔兰庫迪 基爾堅尼高萊安。

nameMeanings.name: Butler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from a word that originally denoted a wine steward, usually the chief servant of a medieval household, from Norman French butuiller (Old French bouteillier, Latin buticularius, from buticula ‘bottle’). In the large households of royalty and the most powerful nobility, the title came to denote an officer of high rank and responsibility, only nominally concerned with the supply of wine, if at all. As well as being widespread in England, this is also the surname of an important Irish family, descended from Theobald FitzWalter, who was appointed Chief Butler of Ireland by King Henry II in 1177. It is Gaelicized as de Buitléir.

English: occasionally perhaps an occupational name from Middle English boteler ‘maker of bottles (usually of leather)’, a derivative of Middle English botel, Old French bo(u)teille ‘bottle’ and synonymous with Botelmaker.
Americanized form of French   Bouthillier   (see   Bouteiller  ).

variants.name: Buttler
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Butler  .
German: occupational name for a village tavern owner, from French bouteillier ‘butler’.
Americanized form of German Buttlar: habitational name from a place so named in Thuringia.

variants.name: Bouthillier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Jacques, Normand, Philippe, Yves.
French: variant of   Bouteiller  . This name is sometimes Americanized as   Butler  . Compare   Boutilier  .

nameMeanings.name: Edmund
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + mund ‘protector’. It was borne by several early royal and saintly figures, including a 9th-century king of East Anglia killed by invading Danes, allegedly for his adherence to Christianity. In the 16th and 17th centuries, there was a good deal of interchange between Edmund and Edward.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Edmund Butler Earl Of Carrick was born in 1271, in Kilkenny, County Kilkenny, Ireland, his father, Theobald Butler 4th Chief Butler of Ireland, was 29 and his mother, Joan FitzJohn, was -33. He married Joan Fitz Gerald Countess Of Carrick in 1295, in Shere, Surrey, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. In 1303, at the age of 32, his occupation is listed as justiciar of ireland in Ireland. He died on 13 September 1321, at the age of 50, and was buried in Gowran, County Kilkenny, Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Edmund Butler Earl Of Carrick est né en 1271, à Kilkenny, County Kilkenny, Irlande, son père, Theobald Butler 4th Chief Butler of Ireland, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Joan FitzJohn, avait -33 ans. Il a épousé Joan Fitz Gerald Countess Of Carrick en 1295, à Shere, Surrey, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. En 1303, à l’âge de 32 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est justiciar of ireland à Irlande. Il est décédé le 13 septembre 1321, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterré à Gowran, County Kilkenny, Irlande.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Edmund Butler Earl Of Carrick 1271 in Kilkenny, County Kilkenny, Irland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Theobald Butler 4th Chief Butler of Ireland 29 und seine Mutter Joan FitzJohn -33. Er hat 1295 in Shere, Surrey, England, United Kingdom Joan Fitz Gerald Countess Of Carrick geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. 1303, als er 32 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als justiciar of ireland in Irland aufgeführt. Er starb am 13. September 1321 im Alter von 50 Jahren und wurde in Gowran, County Kilkenny, Irland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Edmund Butler Earl Of Carrick è nato il nel 1271 nel Kilkenny, County Kilkenny, Irlanda, il suo padre, Theobald Butler 4th Chief Butler of Ireland, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Joan FitzJohn, aveva -33 anni. Lui sposato Joan Fitz Gerald Countess Of Carrick nel 1295, nel Shere, Surrey, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. Nel 1303, all'età di 32 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come justiciar of ireland nel Irlanda. Morì il 13 settembre 1321, all'età di 50 anni, e fu sepolto a Gowran, County Kilkenny, Irlanda.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Edmund Butler Earl Of Carrickが1271年にアイルランドコウントイ キルケニーキルケニーで生まれた時，父Theobald Butler 4th Chief Butler of Irelandは29歳，母Joan FitzJohnは-33歳であった。1295年にイギリスイギリススリーシェアでJoan Fitz Gerald Countess Of Carrickと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。1303年，32歳の時，アイルランドでの職業はjusticiar of irelandと記録されている。彼は1321年9月13日に50歳で死去し，アイルランドコウントイ キルケニーゴウランにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Edmund Butler Earl Of Carrick(이)가 1271년 아일랜드 카운티 킬켄이 킬켄이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Theobald Butler 4th Chief Butler of Ireland(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan FitzJohn(은)는 -33세였다. 그는 1295년 영국 잉글랜드 수리 셔에서 Joan Fitz Gerald Countess Of Carrick(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 1303년, 32세의 나이로 그의 직업은 아일랜드에서 justiciar of ireland이었다. 그는 1321년 9월 13일 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 아일랜드 카운티 킬켄이 가란에 묻혔다.

mn.Shere, Surrey, Farmbridge, Essex, Englan: Shere, Sūþrīge, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Edmund Butler Earl Of Carrick nasceu em 1271, em Kilkenny, County Kilkenny, Irlanda, seu pai, Theobald Butler 4th Chief Butler of Ireland, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Joan FitzJohn, tinha -33 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan Fitz Gerald Countess Of Carrick em 1295, em Shere, Surrey, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. Em 1303, com 32 anos, sua profissão aparece como justiciar of ireland em Irlanda. Ele faleceu em 13 de setembro de 1321, com 50 anos, e foi sepultado em Gowran, County Kilkenny, Irlanda.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Edmund Butler Earl Of Carrick родился в 1271 г. в местности килкенни, коунти килкенни, Ирландия, его отцу по имени Theobald Butler 4th Chief Butler of Ireland было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan FitzJohn, -33. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Fitz Gerald Countess Of Carrick в 1295 г. в местности шир, сурри, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. В 1303 году, в возрасте 32 лет,  он работал по профессии justiciar of ireland в местности Ирландия. Он умер 13 сентября 1321 г. в возрасте 50 лет и был похоронен в местности гоуран, коунти килкенни, Ирландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Edmund Butler Earl Of Carrick nació en 1271, en Kilkenny, County Kilkenny, Irlanda, su papá, Theobald Butler 4th Chief Butler of Ireland, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Joan FitzJohn, tenía -33 años. Se casó con Joan Fitz Gerald Countess Of Carrick en 1295, en Shere, Surrey, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas. En 1303, a la edad de 32 años, su ocupación registrada era justiciar of ireland en Irlanda. Murió el 13 de septiembre de 1321, a la edad de 50 años, y fue sepultado en Gowran, County Kilkenny, Irlanda.
